threats.title: JWT Algorithm Confusion — alg:none and RS256→HS256 Attack
threats.scenario: express-jwt@0.1.3 and jsonwebtoken@0.4.0 predate algorithm confusion fixes. Attacker may submit JWT with alg:none or downgrade to HS256 (using public key as HMAC secret). Older library versions vulnerable to CVE-2022-23529 family.
threats.controls_in_place: RS256 configured but no algorithm allowlist in old library version

threats.title: Missing JWT Claim Validation (iss, aud)
threats.scenario: express-jwt@0.1.3 does not validate issuer or audience claims. A JWT issued by a different service or a forged token without these claims is accepted as valid. Enables token replay across services.

threats.title: Log File Publicly Accessible — Evidence Tampering
threats.scenario: Access logs at /support/logs served publicly (server.ts:281). An attacker who has compromised the system can monitor their detection status, and a privileged attacker with write access could destroy forensic evidence.
threats.controls_in_place: Logs written via morgan to local file

steps: Forward logs to external tamper-resistant SIEM

threats.title: No Rate Limiting on WebSocket Connections
threats.scenario: Socket.IO at /socket.io accepts unlimited WebSocket upgrade requests. Attacker opens thousands of persistent connections exhausting file descriptors and memory in the Node.js process.

steps: Configure Socket.IO allowRequest callback with connection rate limiting
